```{r}
    dplyr::select(co2_trip_emissions_eu, co2_trip_emissions_gfw)

  # Calculate performance metrics
  comparison_results <- filtered_df |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      -co2_trip_emissions_eu,
      names_to = "model",
      values_to = "estimate"
    ) |>
    dplyr::group_by(model) |>
    validation_performance_metrics(
      truth = co2_trip_emissions_eu,
      estimate = estimate
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(-.estimator) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = .metric, values_from = .estimate)

  # Return results with added threshold information
  comparison_results <- comparison_results |>
    dplyr::mutate(threshold = threshold, n_observations = nrow(filtered_df))

  return(comparison_results)
}

# Range of thresholds to test
thresholds <- seq(0.05, 0.4, 0.05)
# Apply calculate_metrics function over range of thresholds
performance_results <- purrr::map_df(
  thresholds,
  ~ calculate_metrics(.x, merged_df)
)

best_performance <- performance_results |>
  dplyr::filter(rsq_trad == max(rsq_trad))

# Select data with threshold of 0.15 the one showing best performance
filtered_hours_df <- subset(merged_df, diff / max < best_performance$threshold)
filtered_df <- filtered_hours_df |>
  dplyr::select(co2_trip_emissions_eu, co2_trip_emissions_gfw)

ggplot(
  filtered_df,
  aes(
    x = co2_trip_emissions_eu,
    y = co2_trip_emissions_gfw
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    size = 1.5,
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "#104E8B"
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = "red",
    linetype = "solid",
    se = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  annotate(
    geom = "text",
    label = paste0(
      "Traditional R² = ",
      format(best_performance$rsq_trad, digits = 2)
    ),
    x = 130000,
    y = 10000,
    size = 3,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1, clip = "on") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, max(filtered_df)), labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(filtered_df)), labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    x = "Published emissions in the EU MRV database (MT)",
    y = "Estimated CO2 emissions using our model (MT)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines"),
    strip.placement = "outside"
  )
```

# Supplementary tables

## Dark fleet model performance metrics 

```{r}
#| results = 'asis'
all_performance_metrics_table <- all_performance_metrics |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    model = stringr::str_replace_all(model, "_", "; ") |>
      stringr::str_to_sentence(),
    test = stringr::str_replace_all(test, "_", " ") |>
      stringr::str_to_sentence()
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    pollutant = stringr::str_to_upper(pollutant) |>
      stringr::str_replace_all("_", "."),
    pollutant = ifelse(pollutant == "VOCS", "VOCs", pollutant),
    pollutant = tidyr::replace_na(pollutant, "-")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    pollutant = forcats::fct_relevel(pollutant, pollutant_factor_levels_tables)
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(pollutant) |>
  dplyr::select(
    Model = model,
    Test = test,
    Pollutant = pollutant,
    Metric = metric,
    Estimate = estimate
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    Metric = dplyr::case_when(
      Metric == "accuracy" ~ "Accuracy",
      Metric == "f_meas" ~ "F1 score",
      Metric == "pr_auc" ~ "Precision-recall AUC",
      Metric == "roc_auc" ~ "ROC AUC",
      Metric == "precision" ~ "Precision",
      Metric == "sensitivity" ~ "Sensitivity",
      Metric == "specificity" ~ "Specificity",
      Metric ==
        "rsq_trad_backtransformed_residuals" ~ "Traditional R² (backtransformed)",
      Metric == "rsq_trad" ~ "Traditional R²",
      Metric == "mae" ~ "MAE",
      Metric ==
        "aggregate_prediction_percent_error" ~ "Percent error of total summed predictions",
      TRUE ~ Metric
    ) |>
      forcats::fct_relevel("Traditional R²")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(Model = forcats::fct_relevel(Model, "Emissions; regression")) |>
  dplyr::arrange(Model, Metric) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(values_from = Estimate, names_from = Test) |>
  knitr::kable(
    format = "latex",
    label = "all_performance_metrics_table",
    caption = "Summary of dark fleet model performance metrics for the: 1) emissions regression model; 2) detections classification mode; and 3) detections regression model. Performance estimates for each metric are shown for both the inside S1 footprint and outside S1 footprint tests. All metrics are calculated at the unit of observation except for 'Percent error of total summed predictions', which is the percentage error in the total sum of all predictions relative to the total sum of all truth values.",
    digits = 2
  ) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(font_size = 8, latex_options = "hold_position")

all_performance_metrics_table

writeLines(
  all_performance_metrics_table,
  here::here("tables/all_performance_metrics_table.tex")
)
```

## Summary of 2017 and 2024 emissions, by pollutant and data source
  
```{r}
total_percent_change_by_fleet_tbl <- total_percent_change_by_fleet |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(
    c(`2017`, `2024`),
    ~ signif(., 3) |>
      prettyNum(big.mark = ",")
  )) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    pollutant = stringr::str_to_upper(pollutant) |>
      stringr::str_replace_all("_", "."),
    pollutant = ifelse(pollutant == "VOCS", "VOCs", pollutant)
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    pollutant = forcats::fct_relevel(pollutant, pollutant_factor_levels_tables)
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      c(percent_total_2024, percent_change),
      ~ scales::percent(., accuracy = 1)
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(pollutant) |>
  dplyr::rename(
    `Data source` = fleet,
    Pollutant = pollutant,
    `2017 emissions (MT)` = `2017`,
    `2024 emissions (MT)` = `2024`,
    `Percent 2024 total` = percent_total_2024,
    `Percent change from 2017 to 2024` = percent_change
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(
    format = "latex",
    label = "total_percent_change_by_fleet",
    caption = "Summary of 2017 and 2024 emissions, by pollutant and data source."
  ) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(font_size = 8, latex_options = "hold_position")

writeLines(
  total_percent_change_by_fleet_tbl,
  here::here("tables/total_percent_change_by_fleet.tex")
)
```

## Underestimation of 2024 emissions using only AIS data, and overestimation of 2017 to 2024 emissions trend using only AIS data, by pollutant.

```{r}
pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary_tbl <- pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    pollutant = stringr::str_to_upper(pollutant) |>
      stringr::str_replace_all("_", "."),
    pollutant = ifelse(pollutant == "VOCS", "VOCs", pollutant)
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    pollutant = forcats::fct_relevel(pollutant, pollutant_factor_levels_tables)
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(pollutant) |>
  dplyr::rename(
    Pollutant = pollutant,
    "AIS-only underestimation of 2024 emissions" = ais_underestimation_2024,
    "AIS-only overestimation of 2017-2024 emissions trend" = trend_overestimation_2017_2024
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(
    format = "latex",
    label = "pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary",
    caption = "Underestimation of 2024 emissions using only AIS data, and overestimation of 2017 to 2024 emissions trend using only AIS data, by pollutant."
  ) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(font_size = 8, latex_options = "hold_position")

writeLines(
  pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary_tbl,
  here::here(
    "tables/pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary.tex"
  )
)
```

### Feature importance

```{r}
#| label: fig-feature-importance
#| fig-cap: Feature importance plots for the three final model fit random forests
#| fig-height: 9
#| fig-width: 7

all_varimp_data |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    data_sources,
    by = c("model_feature" = "Model feature shorthand")
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(model_feature_long = `Model feature`) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    model_feature_long = dplyr::case_when(
      stringr::str_detect(
        model_feature,
        "length_size_bin_"
      ) ~ glue::glue(
        "S1 detections per km2 (length bin {stringr::str_remove(model_feature, 'length_size_bin_')})"
      ),
      TRUE ~ model_feature_long
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(model_feature_long)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    model = stringr::str_replace_all(model, "_", " ") |>
      stringr::str_to_sentence() |>
      forcats::fct_relevel(
        "Detections classification",
        "Detections regression",
        "Emissions regression"
      ),
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = importance,
    y = tidytext::reorder_within(model_feature_long, importance, model)
  )) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    x = "Feature importance score (Gini impurtity corrected)",
    y = "",
  ) +
  theme_plot() +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  facet_wrap(model ~ ., ncol = 1, scales = "free")
```

### Classification model performance

```{r}
#| label: fig-pr-curves
#| fig-cap: Precision recall curves for the detection classification model, shown for the inside S1 footprint and outside S1 footprint performance tests
#| fig-height: 3

performance_detections_cls_roc_pr_curves |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "metric", values_from = "estimate") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    test = stringr::str_replace_all(test, "_", " ") |> stringr::str_to_title()
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = recall, y = precision)) +
  geom_line() +
  coord_equal() +
  facet_wrap(test ~ ., ) +
  labs(
    x = "Recall",
    y = "Precision",
  ) +
  theme_plot()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-roc-curves
#| fig-cap: ROC curves for the detection classification model, shown for the inside S1 footprint and outside S1 footprint performance tests
#| fig-height: 3

performance_detections_cls_roc_pr_curves |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "metric", values_from = "estimate") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    test = stringr::str_replace_all(test, "_", " ") |> stringr::str_to_title()
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity)) +
  geom_line() +
  coord_equal() +
  facet_wrap(test ~ ., ) +
  labs(
    x = "False positive rate (1 - specificity)",
    y = "True positive rate (sensitivity)",
  ) +
  theme_plot() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1))
```


```{r}
#| label: fig-conf-mat
#| fig-cap: Confusion matricies for the detection classification model, shown for the inside S1 footprint and outside S1 footprint performance tests
#| fig-height: 3

performance_detections_cls_conf_mat |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    test = stringr::str_replace_all(test, "_", " ") |> stringr::str_to_title()
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(prediction = forcats::fct_rev(as.factor(prediction))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = truth, y = prediction)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = count), color = "black") +
  geom_label(aes(label = prettyNum(count, big.mark = ",")), vjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "gray95", high = "gray10", guide = "none") +
  facet_wrap(test ~ .) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(x = "Truth", y = "Prediction") +
  theme_minimal()
```


### Dynamic AIS - annual emissions and vessels by AIS receiver type

```{r}
#| label: fig-annual-emissions-by-ais-receiver-type
#| fig-cap: Annual total CO2 emissions (metric tonnes) by the AIS-broadcasting fleet, disaggregated by AIS receiver type (satellite, terrestrial, and dynamic) and total aggregated across receiver types. A dashed vertical line is shown for 2022, the first year in which dynamic AIS was widely adopted.
# | #| fig-width: 4
#| fig-height: 7
annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type <- annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type |>
  # Don't include unknown receiver type
  dplyr::filter(receiver_type != "unknown") |>
  # Fill in missing values with 0s
  tidyr::complete(
    receiver_type,
    tidyr::nesting(year),
    fill = list(emissions_co2_mt = 0, n_distinct_ssvid = 0, number_pings = 0)
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(receiver_type = stringr::str_to_sentence(receiver_type)) |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type |>
  dplyr::rename(
    `CO[2]~emissions~(MT)` = emissions_co2_mt,
    `Number~unique~vessels` = n_distinct_ssvid,
    `Number~AIS~messages` = number_pings,
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -c(year, receiver_type)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, color = receiver_type)) +
  facet_wrap(name ~ ., scales = "free_y", ncol = 1, labeller = label_parsed) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "M", scale = 1e-6),
    limits = c(0, NA)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = unique(annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type$year)
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(
    type = "qual",
    palette = "Set2",
    guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)
  ) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "", y = "", color = "AIS receiver type") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2022, linetype = 2)
```

### Dynamic AIS - annual emissions by AIS receiver type for top flags

```{r}
#| label: fig-annual-emissions-by-ais-receiver-type-top-flags
#| fig-cap: Annual total CO2 emissions (metric tonnes) by the AIS-broadcasting fleet, disaggregated by AIS receiver type (satellite, terrestrial, and dynamic) and total aggregated across receiver types, for the top 10 flags with the most emissions in 2024. A dashed vertical line is shown for 2022, the first year in which dynamic AIS was widely adopted.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 6
annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag <- annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag |>
  # Add column name for country name based on flag

  dplyr::mutate(
    flag = countrycode::countrycode(
      flag,
      origin = "iso3c",
      destination = "country.name"
    )
  ) |>
  # Don't include unknown receiver type
  dplyr::filter(receiver_type != "unknown") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    receiver_type = stringr::str_to_sentence(receiver_type) |>
      forcats::fct_relevel("Satellite", "Terrestrial", "Dynamic")
  ) |>
  # Fill in missing values with 0s
  tidyr::complete(
    receiver_type,
    tidyr::nesting(year, flag),
    fill = list(emissions_co2_mt = 0, n_distinct_ssvid = 0)
  ) |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

top_n_flags_2024 <- annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2024, receiver_type == "Total") |>
  dplyr::slice_max(n = 10, order_by = emissions_co2_mt) |>
  dplyr::select(flag)

annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag |>
  dplyr::inner_join(top_n_flags_2024, by = "flag") |>
  dplyr::mutate(flag = forcats::fct_relevel(flag, top_n_flags_2024$flag)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = emissions_co2_mt, color = flag)) +
  facet_grid(receiver_type ~ ., scales = "free_y") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "M", scale = 1e-6)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = unique(annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag$year)
  ) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "", y = expression(CO[2] ~ emissions ~ (MT)), color = "Flag") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2022, linetype = 2) +
  paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d(palette = "rcartocolor::Safe")

```

### Dynamic AIS - map showing spatial change in emissions by AIS receiver type

```{r}
#| label: fig-co2-emissions-change-map-by-receiver-type
#| fig-cap: Spatial change in CO2 emissions by the AIS-broadcasting fleet between 2017 and 2024 (metric tonnes) by receiver type (satellite, terrestrial, and dynamic) and total across receiver types, at a 1x1 degree spatial resolution.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 8
# Annual spatial CO2 emissions vt receiver type  (satellite; dynamic; or terrestrial)
annual_spatial_emissions_by_receiver_type <- annual_spatial_emissions_by_receiver_type |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    receiver_type = stringr::str_to_sentence(receiver_type) |>
      forcats::fct_relevel("Satellite", "Terrestrial", "Dynamic")
  ) |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    emissions_mt_per_km2 = emissions_co2_mt / lat_to_area(lat_bin)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-emissions_co2_mt)

annual_spatial_emissions_by_receiver_type |>
  dplyr::filter(year %in% c(analysis_start_year, analysis_end_year)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = emissions_mt_per_km2) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(c(`2017`, `2024`), ~ tidyr::replace_na(., 0))) |>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_mt_per_km2 = `2024` - `2017`) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_bbox_sf, fill = "grey90") +
  ggspatial::geom_spatial_tile(aes(
    x = lon_bin + 0.5,
    y = lat_bin + 0.5,
    fill = emissions_mt_per_km2,
    color = emissions_mt_per_km2
  )) +
  geom_sf(data = land, color = "#374a6d", fill = "#374a6d") +
  facet_wrap(receiver_type ~ ., ncol = 1) +
  scico::scale_fill_scico(
    expression(atop(
      Change ~ of ~ CO[2] ~ emissions,
      from ~ 2017 ~ to ~ 2024 ~ (MT / km^2)
    )),
    palette = "vik",
    midpoint = 0,
    trans = ggallin::pseudolog10_trans,
    na.value = "grey90",
    breaks = c(-1e3, -1e2, -1e1, 0, 1e1, 1e2, 1e3),
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  scico::scale_color_scico(
    expression(atop(
      Change ~ of ~ CO[2] ~ emissions,
      from ~ 2017 ~ to ~ 2024 ~ (MT / km^2)
    )),
    palette = "vik",
    midpoint = 0,
    trans = ggallin::pseudolog10_trans,
    na.value = "grey90",
    breaks = c(-1e3, -1e2, -1e1, 0, 1e1, 1e2, 1e3),
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right", legend.direction = "vertical") +
  guides(
    fill = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      frame.colour = "black",
      ticks.colour = "black",
      barwidth = unit(1, "cm"),
      barheight = unit(5, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 1)) +
  coord_sf(crs = map_projection)
```

## MRV performance validation table

```{r}
performance_results |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    rsq = signif(rsq, 2),
    rsq_trad = signif(rsq_trad, 2),
    n_observations = prettyNum(n_observations, big.mark = ",")
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    Tolerance = threshold,
    `Number of vessels` = n_observations,
    "R$^{2}$" = rsq,
    "Traditional R$^{2}$" = rsq_trad
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(
    format = "latex",
    label = "tab-performance-tolerance-table",
    caption = "Performance summary across different thresholds for the hours at sea tolerance between datasets. Results are shown using the R$^{2}$ (measuring linear association between observed and predicted values) and traditional R$^{2}$ (proportion of variance explained compared to the mean-only baseline).",
    escape = FALSE
  ) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(font_size = 8, latex_options = "hold_position") |>
  writeLines(
    here::here("tables/mrv_performance_results.tex")
  )
```


# Non-broadcasting model data sources


```{r}
data_sources_table <- data_sources |>
  dplyr::select(-`Model feature shorthand`) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      dplyr::everything(),
      ~ stringr::str_replace_all(., "²", "$^2$")
    )
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(
    format = "latex",
    escape = FALSE,
    booktabs = TRUE,
    longtable = FALSE,
    caption = "Data sources for all included model features, including which random forest model uses the features (detections classification; detections regression; and/or emissions regression), the units, feature type, whether it varies spatially, whether it varies temporally, and the source name and reference citation.",
    label = "data_sources"
  ) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position"),
    font_size = 7
  ) |>
  kableExtra::landscape()

writeLines(data_sources_table, here::here("tables/data_sources.tex"))
```


```{r}
#| label: fig-offshore-outside-footprint-training-testing-map
#| fig-cap: Maps of pixels that were used in the training and testing datasets for the outside S1 performance tests for each model. Pixels <=100km from the shore were used for training; pixels >100km from shore were used for testing. The exact training/testing split for each of the 3 models is shown.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 7.5
pixels_for_offshore_training_testing_split |>
  dplyr::mutate(type = ifelse(offshore_pixel, "Testing", "Training")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    model = stringr::str_replace_all(model, "_", "; ") |>
      stringr::str_to_sentence()
  ) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_bbox_sf, fill = "grey90") +
  ggspatial::geom_spatial_tile(
    aes(
      x = lon_bin + 0.5,
      y = lat_bin + 0.5,
      color = type,
      fill = type
    )
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = land, color = "#374a6d", fill = "#374a6d") +
  paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d(
    name = "Dataset",
    palette = "nbapalettes::pacers_classic",
    direction = -1
  ) +
  paletteer::scale_fill_paletteer_d(
    name = "Dataset",
    palette = "nbapalettes::pacers_classic",
    direction = -1
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_void() +
  theme_plot() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "horizontal") +
  facet_wrap(model ~ ., ncol = 1) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-map-fraction-months-imaged
#| fig-cap: Map showing the fraction of months from 2017 through 2024 that were imaged by S1 for each 1x1 degree pixel.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 5
number_s1_imaged_months_by_pixel |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    fraction_months_imaged = number_months_imaged / number_months
  ) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_bbox_sf, fill = "grey90") +
  ggspatial::geom_spatial_tile(
    aes(
      x = lon_bin + 0.5,
      y = lat_bin + 0.5,
      color = fraction_months_imaged,
      fill = fraction_months_imaged
    )
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = land, color = "#374a6d", fill = "#374a6d") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Percentage of months imaged by S1 between 2017 and 2024",
    labels = scales::percent,
    option = "magma"
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    name = "Percentage of months imaged by S1 between 2017 and 2024",
    labels = scales::percent,
    option = "magma"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_void() +
  theme_plot() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "horizontal") +
  guides(
    fill = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      frame.colour = "black",
      ticks.colour = "white",
      barwidth = unit(15, "cm"),
      barheight = unit(1, "cm")
    ),
    color = "none"
  )
```
